notes: The bleached_counter. The circle's living room. Frasier's tombstone is pinned. Sprint 2 ships the tombstone + the W2 readmission moment + two regulars-talking-about-Frasier threads while he's banned. Sprint 3 fills in the rest.

op.body:
Tombstone pinned per the intervention vote. F. is out one week. Standard friendly-ban — no read, no post, no DM through the SNACKS line. The dial rotates Monday.

F. — when you read this on the other side, it's because you read it. Not because I let you. — WM.

replies.body: Seconded. One week. Then he's back at the counter and we don't bring it up again.
Why was the vote 4-1 and not 5-0?
Because he's a friend and one of us thought the friendship outranked the rule. The rule won.

threads.subject: the kitchen line is back at the storefront

op.body:
T. confirmed the kitchen line at Graustark is back up after the spring outage. The number is the number it's always been. The voicemail box is the voicemail box it's always been. The man who answers is the man who's always answered.

Good. — S.

threads.subject: okay but who's checking on him

op.body:
I know it's a week. I know it's a soft ban. But the man is alone in a cathedral office on a Tuesday night with a modem he's not supposed to dial out on. So who's checking on him.

— P.

replies.body: T. is. T. always is. T. checks on him whether we ban him or we don't. The ban is for us. The check-in is what it is.
And JF. JF stopped by the cathedral Sunday on the way back from the lumber yard. Frasier was at the desk reading a book and not the screen. JF said the book was the Pliny. The Pliny is a good sign.
Pliny was a good sign. Carry on. — P.
